raw_text:
ERROR   [TC-G-002] interface.f90:5 <-> header.h:3  param[0] 'handler'
  FUNPTR_VS_PTR: C parameter is a function pointer (event_handler).
  Fortran declares TYPE(c_ptr) (data pointer). Fortran 2003 §15.3.7:
  c_ptr and c_funptr are distinct types. On architectures where code and
  data pointers differ in size (e.g., DOS far model, some DSPs), this
  causes immediate crash. On x86-64 sizes coincide but it is still
  non-conforming and may fail with aggressive optimisation.

NOTE: Use TYPE(c_funptr) for all function pointer interop.
